text: Paul greets the church and addresses divisions among believers. He emphasizes that the message of the cross, though foolishness to the world, is the power of God for salvation. God chooses what is weak and despised to display His glory, so that no one may boast before Him.

Paul reminds the Corinthians that his message was not based on human wisdom but on the Spirit’s power. Spiritual truths are revealed by the Holy Spirit, and only those who are spiritual can discern them. Believers have the mind of Christ.

The Corinthians are rebuked for spiritual immaturity and division. Paul explains that ministers are merely servants, and God gives the growth. Believers are God’s field and building, and Christ is the only true foundation.

Paul describes apostles as servants and stewards of God’s mysteries. He warns against pride and reminds them that all they have is from God. He contrasts true apostolic suffering with the Corinthians’ self-satisfaction.
Paul confronts serious sin in the church and calls for discipline. He instructs the church to remove unrepentant sin from among them to preserve purity and holiness.
